examples: Dirges so melancholic that they would scorch you.
The most renowned Portuguese music is Fado, a form of melancholic music.
As he talks, he recognises the irony of the story and his melancholic face breaks into a quick laugh.
The beautiful, melancholic story of Liang Shanbo and Zhu Yingtai has mesmerized Chinese for centuries.
Spanning 60 years, Anne Tyler's novel is a melancholic story of a mismatched marriage.
